ddls:
CREATE TABLE "majors" (
  "major_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each major */,
  "major_name" TEXT /* Name of the major */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the major */,
  "degree_level" TEXT /* Degree level (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, Doctorate) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department offering the major */,
  "program_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the program in years */,
  "is_interdisciplinary" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the major is interdisciplinary */,
  "median_salary" TEXT /* Median starting salary for graduates */,
  "job_satisfaction" TEXT /* Average job satisfaction score */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("major_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "institutions" (
  "institution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each institution */,
  "institution_name" TEXT /* Name of the institution */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the institution */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of institution (e.g., Public, Private, Community College) */,
  "established_year" INTEGER /* Year the institution was established */,
  "total_students" INTEGER /* Total number of students */,
  "total_faculty" INTEGER /* Total number of faculty members */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the institution's website */,
  "accreditation" TEXT /* Accreditation status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("institution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_data" (
  "employment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment data entry */,
  "major_id" INTEGER /* ID of the major */,
  "institution_id" INTEGER /* ID of the institution */,
  "total_graduates" INTEGER /* Total number of graduates */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Sample size of graduates */,
  "employed" INTEGER /* Number of employed graduates */,
  "unemployed" INTEGER /* Number of unemployed graduates */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the data */,
  "employment_rate" TEXT /* Calculated employment rate */,
  "unemployment_rate" TEXT /* Calculated unemployment rate */,
  "part_time_employed" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed part-time */,
  "full_time_employed" INTEGER /* Number of graduates employed full-time */,
  "not_seeking_employment" INTEGER /* Number of graduates not seeking employment */,
  "avg_salary" TEXT /* Average salary of employed graduates */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_institution_id FOREIGN KEY ("institution_id") REFERENCES institutions ("institution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_major_id FOREIGN KEY ("major_id") REFERENCES majors ("major_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, researcher, policymaker) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date */,
  "status" TEXT /* User status (active, inactive, suspended) */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email of the contact person */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last date the source was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_imports" (
  "import_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data import */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "institution_id" INTEGER /* ID of the institution */,
  "import_date" TEXT /* Date the data was imported */,
  "data_file" TEXT /* File path to the imported data file */,
  "import_status" TEXT /* Status of the import (success, failed) */,
  "imported_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who imported the data */,
  "imported_records" INTEGER /* Number of records imported */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("import_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_institution_id FOREIGN KEY ("institution_id") REFERENCES institutions ("institution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_imported_by FOREIGN KEY ("imported_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path to the report file */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the report */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Last date the report was modified */,
  "access_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the report has been accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rankings" (
  "ranking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ranking entry */,
  "major_id" INTEGER /* ID of the major */,
  "ranking" INTEGER /* Ranking of the major */,
  "ranking_date" TEXT /* Date the ranking was calculated */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the ranking */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source used for ranking */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ranking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rankings_major_id FOREIGN KEY ("major_id") REFERENCES majors ("major_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rankings_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the database was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Session ID of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object detailing specific permissions for the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
